title: The Master's Shortcut
description: Two publications sit on the board. The clues contradict both — prove it, then plan the master mixes that expose them.

hints.text: A master debunk can expose a publication for ingredient X if the OTHER ingredient in the mix is definitively known — the audience computes the expected result using the known alchemical and the publication's claimed alchemical, then compares it to the true result. ing1 is fully pinned down by three aspect clues. Which publication-holders can a master mix of ing1 expose?

ing1 is known to be npN. The publication for ing3 claims Nnp — if correct, mix(npN, Nnp) = R-. But ing3 has R+ (from the clue), so every alchemical consistent with that clue (pNn, Ppn, PPP, pnP) gives a different result with npN: B-, B-, G+, neutral. The prediction R- is guaranteed to be wrong — the publication will be exposed. The same logic applies to ing5: R- is known for ing5, and every consistent alchemical gives a result other than the publication's predicted B-.

Step 1: Master debunk — mix ing1 + ing3. ing1 is npN; the claimed alch for ing3 (Nnp) predicts R-, but the true result is B- → ing3's publication removed. Step 2: Master debunk — mix ing1 + ing5. The claimed alch for ing5 (pNn) predicts B-, but the true result is R- → ing5's publication removed.
